title: Actions, preconditions and effects deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap, and deterministic offline Python lab for Actions, preconditions and effects; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Actions, preconditions and effects: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? A list of actions is a valid plan whenever its final action mentions the goal, regardless of intermediate preconditions.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Simulate actions from the initial state, require every precondition when used, apply complete effects, and test the explicit goal after the full sequence.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Simulate actions from the initial state, require every precondition when used, apply complete effects, and test the explicit goal after the full sequence.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Simulate actions from the initial state, require every precondition when used, apply complete effects, and test the explicit goal after the full sequence.
A list of actions is a valid plan whenever its final action mentions the goal, regardless of intermediate preconditions.

answer.explanation: Simulate actions from the initial state, require every precondition when used, apply complete effects, and test the explicit goal after the full sequence.

items.title: Actions, preconditions and effects: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'every precondition'. Simulate actions from the initial state, require every precondition when used, apply complete effects, and test the explicit goal after the full sequence.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Simulate actions from the initial state, require every precondition when used, apply complete effects, and test the explicit goal after the full sequence.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Simulate actions from the initial state, require [[checkpoint]] when used, apply complete effects, and test the explicit goal after the full sequence.

answer.explanation: Simulate actions from the initial state, require every precondition when used, apply complete effects, and test the explicit goal after the full sequence.

items.title: Actions, preconditions and effects: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read starting energy and a whitespace action sequence using move and charge. move requires energy, advances position, and spends one; charge adds one. Print valid only if every action applies and final position is at least two.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

answer.explanation: The validator checks each precondition before applying effects and evaluates the explicit goal only after the whole sequence.
